<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[en:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[ja:雪野山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11659952]] Wikidata name: Yukinoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Kansai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Yukinoyama Kofun | native_name = 雪野山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yukinoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07645|N|136.14581944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]], [[:en:Ryūō, Shiga|Ryūō, Shiga]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. | region = [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yukinoyama Kofun'''|雪野山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located on the border the cities of [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]] and the town of [[:en:Ryūō, Shiga| Ryūō]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2014.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/241975 |title=雪野山古墳|trans-title= Yukinoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yukinoyama Kofun is located at the 200 meter elevation point on Mount Yukino, a 308.82 meter mountain on the Koto Plain on the southeastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the north-northeast, or parallel to Lake Biwa. It has an overall length of 70 meters, and was built in two-tiers and covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been discovered. The tumulus makes use of the natural terrain of the hill in that the lower portion of the posterior circular portion and front of the anterior rectangular portion are carved directly out of the [[:en:rhyolite|rhyolite]] stone of the mountain. The tumulus was discovered only in the summer of 1989, as the tumulus had been remodeled and reused during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] as the site of Yukinoyama Castle, which used the posterior circular portion as its [[:en:Inner bailey|Inner bailey]]. From 1989 to 1992, four [[archaeological excavation]]s had been completed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These excavations found two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s lined up in the east and west at the top of the posterior circular portion. The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]] in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 40 meter diameter x 4.5 meter high x 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 2.5 meters high x 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[https://e-omi-muse.com/maibun/yukinoyama/index.html Higashiomi City homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}}

size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yukinoyama Kofun'''|雪野山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located on the border the cities of [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]] and the town of [[:en:Ryūō, Shiga| Ryūō]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2014.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/241975 |title=雪野山古墳|trans-title= Yukinoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yukinoyama Kofun is located at the 200 meter elevation point on Mount Yukino, a 308.82 meter mountain on the Koto Plain on the southeastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the north-northeast, or parallel to Lake Biwa. It has an overall length of 70 meters, and was built in two-tiers and covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been discovered. The tumulus makes use of the natural terrain of the hill in that the lower portion of the posterior circular portion and front of the anterior rectangular portion are carved directly out of the [[:en:rhyolite|rhyolite]] stone of the mountain. The tumulus was discovered only in the summer of 1989, as the tumulus had been remodeled and reused during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] as the site of Yukinoyama Castle, which used the posterior circular portion as its [[:en:Inner bailey|Inner bailey]]. From 1989 to 1992, four [[archaeological excavation]]s had been completed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These excavations found two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s lined up in the east and west at the top of the posterior circular portion. The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]] in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 40 meter diameter x 4.5 meter high x 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 2.5 meters high x 2-tier

size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yukinoyama Kofun'''|雪野山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located on the border the cities of [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]] and the town of [[:en:Ryūō, Shiga| Ryūō]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2014.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/241975 |title=雪野山古墳|trans-title= Yukinoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yukinoyama Kofun is located at the 200 meter elevation point on Mount Yukino, a 308.82 meter mountain on the Koto Plain on the southeastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the north-northeast, or parallel to Lake Biwa. It has an overall length of 70 meters, and was built in two-tiers and covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been discovered. The tumulus makes use of the natural terrain of the hill in that the lower portion of the posterior circular portion and front of the anterior rectangular portion are carved directly out of the [[:en:rhyolite|rhyolite]] stone of the mountain. The tumulus was discovered only in the summer of 1989, as the tumulus had been remodeled and reused during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] as the site of Yukinoyama Castle, which used the posterior circular portion as its [[:en:Inner bailey|Inner bailey]]. From 1989 to 1992, four [[archaeological excavation]]s had been completed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These excavations found two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s lined up in the east and west at the top of the posterior circular portion. The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]] in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 40 meter diameter x 4.5 meter high x 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 2.5 meters high x 2-tier

size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yukinoyama Kofun'''|雪野山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located on the border the cities of [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]] and the town of [[:en:Ryūō, Shiga| Ryūō]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], in the [[:en:Kansai region|Kansai region]] of [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2014.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/241975 |title=雪野山古墳|trans-title= Yukinoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yukinoyama Kofun is located at the 200 meter elevation point on Mount Yukino, a 308.82 meter mountain on the Koto Plain on the southeastern shore of [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]]. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the north-northeast, or parallel to Lake Biwa. It has an overall length of 70 meters, and was built in two-tiers and covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. No ''[[:en:Haniwa|Haniwa]]'' have been discovered. The tumulus makes use of the natural terrain of the hill in that the lower portion of the posterior circular portion and front of the anterior rectangular portion are carved directly out of the [[:en:rhyolite|rhyolite]] stone of the mountain. The tumulus was discovered only in the summer of 1989, as the tumulus had been remodeled and reused during the [[:en:Sengoku period|Sengoku period]] as the site of Yukinoyama Castle, which used the posterior circular portion as its [[:en:Inner bailey|Inner bailey]]. From 1989 to 1992, four [[archaeological excavation]]s had been completed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These excavations found two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s lined up in the east and west at the top of the posterior circular portion. The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The [[:en:Sarcophagus|Sarcophagus]] is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of [[:en:Grave goods|Grave goods]] was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three [[:en:Bronze mirror|Bronze mirror]]s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|National Important Cultural Property]] in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 40 meter diameter x 4.5 meter high x 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 2.5 meters high x 2-tier

name =Yukinoyama Kofun | native_name = 雪野山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yukinoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07645|N|136.14581944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]], {{ill|Ryūō, Shiga|en|Ryūō, Shiga|WD=Q1087560|lt=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yukinoyama Kofun'''|雪野山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located on the border the cities of [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]] and the town of {{ill|Ryūō, Shiga|en|Ryūō, Shiga|WD=Q1087560|lt= Ryūō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2014.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/241975 |title=雪野山古墳|trans-title= Yukinoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yukinoyama Kofun is located at the 200 meter elevation point on Mount Yukino, a 308.82 meter mountain on the Koto Plain on the southeastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa}}. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the north-northeast, or parallel to Lake Biwa. It has an overall length of 70 meters, and was built in two-tiers and covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been discovered. The tumulus makes use of the natural terrain of the hill in that the lower portion of the posterior circular portion and front of the anterior rectangular portion are carved directly out of the {{ill|rhyolite|en|rhyolite|WD=Q190727|lt=rhyolite}} stone of the mountain. The tumulus was discovered only in the summer of 1989, as the tumulus had been remodeled and reused during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} as the site of Yukinoyama Castle, which used the posterior circular portion as its {{ill|Inner bailey|en|Inner bailey|WD=Q381634|lt=Inner bailey}}. From 1989 to 1992, four [[archaeological excavation]]s had been completed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These excavations found two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s lined up in the east and west at the top of the posterior circular portion. The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property}} in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 40 meter diameter x 4.5 meter high x 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 2.5 meters high x 2-tier

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[en:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[ja:雪野山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[en:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[ja:雪野山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[en:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[ja:雪野山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11659952]] Wikidata name: Yukinoyama Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Kofun in Kansai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Yukinoyama Kofun | native_name = 雪野山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yukinoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07645|N|136.14581944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]], {{ill|Ryūō, Shiga|en|Ryūō, Shiga|WD=Q1087560|lt=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yukinoyama Kofun'''|雪野山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located on the border the cities of [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]] and the town of {{ill|Ryūō, Shiga|en|Ryūō, Shiga|WD=Q1087560|lt= Ryūō}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2014.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/241975 |title=雪野山古墳|trans-title= Yukinoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yukinoyama Kofun is located at the 200 meter elevation point on Mount Yukino, a 308.82 meter mountain on the Koto Plain on the southeastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa}}. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the north-northeast, or parallel to Lake Biwa. It has an overall length of 70 meters, and was built in two-tiers and covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa}}'' have been discovered. The tumulus makes use of the natural terrain of the hill in that the lower portion of the posterior circular portion and front of the anterior rectangular portion are carved directly out of the {{ill|rhyolite|en|rhyolite|WD=Q190727|lt=rhyolite}} stone of the mountain. The tumulus was discovered only in the summer of 1989, as the tumulus had been remodeled and reused during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period}} as the site of Yukinoyama Castle, which used the posterior circular portion as its {{ill|Inner bailey|en|Inner bailey|WD=Q381634|lt=Inner bailey}}. From 1989 to 1992, four [[archaeological excavation]]s had been completed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These excavations found two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s lined up in the east and west at the top of the posterior circular portion. The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property}} in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 40 meter diameter x 4.5 meter high x 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 2.5 meters high x 2-tier

The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus}} is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods}} was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror}}s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property}} in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[en:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[ja:雪野山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[co:commons:Category:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[en:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[ja:雪野山古墳]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[sn:commons:Category:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[en:Yukinoyama Kofun]][[ja:雪野山古墳]]
{{Short description|Kofun in Kansai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Yukinoyama Kofun | native_name = 雪野山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yukinoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07645|N|136.14581944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]], {{ill|Ryūō, Shiga|en|Ryūō, Shiga|WD=Q1087560|lt=Ryūō, Shiga|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yukinoyama Kofun'''|雪野山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located on the border the cities of [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]] and the town of {{ill|Ryūō, Shiga|en|Ryūō, Shiga|WD=Q1087560|lt= Ryūō|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2014.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/241975 |title=雪野山古墳|trans-title= Yukinoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yukinoyama Kofun is located at the 200 meter elevation point on Mount Yukino, a 308.82 meter mountain on the Koto Plain on the southeastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the north-northeast, or parallel to Lake Biwa. It has an overall length of 70 meters, and was built in two-tiers and covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been discovered. The tumulus makes use of the natural terrain of the hill in that the lower portion of the posterior circular portion and front of the anterior rectangular portion are carved directly out of the {{ill|rhyolite|en|rhyolite|WD=Q190727|lt=rhyolite|1=Rhyolite}} stone of the mountain. The tumulus was discovered only in the summer of 1989, as the tumulus had been remodeled and reused during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} as the site of Yukinoyama Castle, which used the posterior circular portion as its {{ill|Inner bailey|en|Inner bailey|WD=Q381634|lt=Inner bailey|1=Inner bailey}}. From 1989 to 1992, four [[archaeological excavation]]s had been completed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These excavations found two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s lined up in the east and west at the top of the posterior circular portion. The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>

The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2014.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/241975 |title=雪野山古墳|trans-title= Yukinoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yukinoyama Kofun is located at the 200 meter elevation point on Mount Yukino, a 308.82 meter mountain on the Koto Plain on the southeastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the north-northeast, or parallel to Lake Biwa. It has an overall length of 70 meters, and was built in two-tiers and covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been discovered. The tumulus makes use of the natural terrain of the hill in that the lower portion of the posterior circular portion and front of the anterior rectangular portion are carved directly out of the {{ill|rhyolite|en|rhyolite|WD=Q190727|lt=rhyolite|1=Rhyolite}} stone of the mountain. The tumulus was discovered only in the summer of 1989, as the tumulus had been remodeled and reused during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} as the site of Yukinoyama Castle, which used the posterior circular portion as its {{ill|Inner bailey|en|Inner bailey|WD=Q381634|lt=Inner bailey|1=Inner bailey}}. From 1989 to 1992, four [[archaeological excavation]]s had been completed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These excavations found two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s lined up in the east and west at the top of the posterior circular portion. The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 40 meter diameter x 4.5 meter high x 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 2.5 meters high x 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}

{{Short description|Kofun in Kansai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name =Yukinoyama Kofun | native_name = 雪野山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yukinoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07645|N|136.14581944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]], {{ill|Ryūō, Shiga|en|Ryūō, Shiga|WD=Q1087560|lt=Ryūō, Shiga|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yukinoyama Kofun'''|雪野山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located on the border the cities of [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]] and the town of {{ill|Ryūō, Shiga|en|Ryūō, Shiga|WD=Q1087560|lt= Ryūō|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2014.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/241975 |title=雪野山古墳|trans-title= Yukinoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yukinoyama Kofun is located at the 200 meter elevation point on Mount Yukino, a 308.82 meter mountain on the Koto Plain on the southeastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the north-northeast, or parallel to Lake Biwa. It has an overall length of 70 meters, and was built in two-tiers and covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been discovered. The tumulus makes use of the natural terrain of the hill in that the lower portion of the posterior circular portion and front of the anterior rectangular portion are carved directly out of the {{ill|rhyolite|en|rhyolite|WD=Q190727|lt=rhyolite|1=Rhyolite}} stone of the mountain. The tumulus was discovered only in the summer of 1989, as the tumulus had been remodeled and reused during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} as the site of Yukinoyama Castle, which used the posterior circular portion as its {{ill|Inner bailey|en|Inner bailey|WD=Q381634|lt=Inner bailey|1=Inner bailey}}. From 1989 to 1992, four [[archaeological excavation]]s had been completed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These excavations found two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s lined up in the east and west at the top of the posterior circular portion. The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>
;Overall length: 70 meters ;Posterior circular portion: 40 meter diameter x 4.5 meter high x 2-tier ;Anterior rectangular portion: 30 meters wide x 2.5 meters high x 2-tier
==See also== *[[List of Historic Sites of Japan (Shiga)]]
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{commons Category:kk]][[Category:}} *[https://e-omi-muse.com/maibun/yukinoyama/index.html Higashiomi City homepage] {{in lang|ja}} {{Kofun navbar}}

It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>

revision.text:
{{Short description|Kofun in Kansai region of Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpō-kōen-fun]] | name =Yukinoyama Kofun | native_name = 雪野山古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Yukinoyama Kofun, funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_type = Japan Shiga Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Yukinoyama Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.07645|N|136.14581944|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]], {{ill|Ryūō, Shiga|en|Ryūō, Shiga|WD=Q1087560|lt=Ryūō, Shiga|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = [[Kofun]] | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = 4th century AD | abandoned = | epochs = [[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (no public facilities) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles = | architectural_details = | notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} }} The {{nihongo|'''Yukinoyama Kofun'''|雪野山古墳||}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[Kofun]] located on the border the cities of [[:en:Ōmihachiman, Shiga|Ōmihachiman]], [[:en:Higashiōmi, Shiga| Higashiōmi]] and the town of {{ill|Ryūō, Shiga|en|Ryūō, Shiga|WD=Q1087560|lt= Ryūō|1=Ryūō, Shiga}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|en|Shiga Prefecture|WD=Q131358|lt=Shiga Prefecture|1=Shiga Prefecture}}, in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|WD=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|WD=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 2014.<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/heritages/detail/241975 |title=雪野山古墳|trans-title= Yukinoyama Kofun|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Overview== The Yukinoyama Kofun is located at the 200 meter elevation point on Mount Yukino, a 308.82 meter mountain on the Koto Plain on the southeastern shore of {{ill|Lake Biwa|en|Lake Biwa|WD=Q200239|lt=Lake Biwa|1=Lake Biwa}}. The tumulus is a {{nihongo|''zenpō-kōen-fun''|前方後円墳|lt=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpō-kōen-fun|1=[[Zenpō-kōen-fun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above, orientated to the north-northeast, or parallel to Lake Biwa. It has an overall length of 70 meters, and was built in two-tiers and covered in ''[[fukiishi]]''. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been discovered. The tumulus makes use of the natural terrain of the hill in that the lower portion of the posterior circular portion and front of the anterior rectangular portion are carved directly out of the {{ill|rhyolite|en|rhyolite|WD=Q190727|lt=rhyolite|1=Rhyolite}} stone of the mountain. The tumulus was discovered only in the summer of 1989, as the tumulus had been remodeled and reused during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|WD=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}} as the site of Yukinoyama Castle, which used the posterior circular portion as its {{ill|Inner bailey|en|Inner bailey|WD=Q381634|lt=Inner bailey|1=Inner bailey}}. From 1989 to 1992, four [[archaeological excavation]]s had been completed.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
These excavations found two [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]]s lined up in the east and west at the top of the posterior circular portion. The pit-type stone chamber on the east side, which is believed to have been the earlier construction, had a main axis from north-to-south, and was made of irregularly shaped lump stones of rhyolite, which was quarried on the mountain itself. The stone chamber was coated with a vermillion paint, and had a clay floor. The {{ill|Sarcophagus|en|Sarcophagus|WD=Q48634|lt=Sarcophagus|1=Sarcophagus}} is estimated to have been a boat-shaped wooden structure, 5.2 meters in length, 0.9 meters in width at the north end, and 0.8 meters or more at the south end, and had two partition plates to divide the interior into three parts.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
A very large amount of {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|WD=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} was recovered from the inside of the burial chamber. The body was buried in the central section of the sarcophagus, accompanied by iron swords wrapped in cloth on both sides. Three {{ill|Bronze mirror|en|Bronze mirror|WD=Q1041984|lt=Bronze mirror|1=Bronze mirror}}s were buried on the north side and two on the south side, of which two on the north side and two on the south side were leaned against the north-south partition with the mirrors facing the burial person. Only one mirror on the north side was arranged mirror side down, for unknown reasons. Beads, including cylindrical and ''[[magatama]]'' beads of jasper were also found. In the northern section of the sarcophagus contained stone products such as arrowheads and fishhooks, and agricultural tools such as sickles, and chisels, and fragments of armor. Outside the casket were the remains of leather helmets, wooden shields, iron swords and spearheads and various shards of pottery. It is estimated that these grave goods date from the early 4th century AD. These artifacts are stored at the {{nihongo|Higashiōmi Buried Artifacts Cultural Center|東近江市埋蔵文化財センター|Higashiōmishi Maizō Bunkazai Sentā}} and were collectively designated an {{ill|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Important Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q1188622|lt=National Important Cultural Property|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}} in 2001.<ref name="Bunka2">{{cite web |url= https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/10496|title=滋賀県雪野山古墳出土品|trans-title= Shiga Prefecture Yukinoyama Ancient Tomb Excavated Items|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}}</ref>
==Gallery== <gallery> 雪野山古墳出土 内行花文鏡 (1号鏡).JPG|Inward Flower Mirror (No. 1 Mirror)） 雪野山古墳出土 だ龍鏡 (2号鏡).JPG|Dragon Mirror (No. 2 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁波文帯盤龍鏡 (3号鏡).JPG|Triangular Margin Wave Band Dragon Mirror (No. 3 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 三角縁唐草文帯四神四獣鏡 (4号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Karakusa Bunka Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 4 Mirror) 雪野山古墳出土 しん出銘三角縁四神四獣鏡 (5号鏡).JPG|Triangular Edge Four Gods Four Beast Mirror (No. 5 Mirror) </gallery>
